title: columnea radicans
intro: Columnea radicans, also known as the creeping columnea, is a type of flowering plant in the family Gesneriaceae. It is a low-growing, evergreen perennial that is native to Central and South America, with medicinal properties derived from its leaves and stems. The plant contains compounds such as flavonoids and alkaloids, which have been traditionally used to treat various health issues, including fever, rheumatism, and digestive problems. Columnea radicans can be prepared in herbal teas, infusions, or tinctures, and has been reported to offer health benefits such as reducing inflammation, improving immune function, and supporting cardiovascular health.
